channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it? - Space
item.description:
1. NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it?  Space
  2. Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. 1st-of-its-kind mission will attempt to save aging space telescope using robot spacecraft  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  4. NASA to save Swift spacecraft from plunging back to Earth  EarthSky
  5. How Virginia powered a 1st-of-its kind space telescope rescue mission  vadogwood.com

item.title: Uranus, Neptune May Be Magma Worlds, Not Ice Giants - Universe Today
item.description:
1. Uranus, Neptune May Be Magma Worlds, Not Ice Giants  Universe Today
  2. Scientists Think Uranus and Neptune May Not Be the Ice Giants We Imagined  Gizmodo
  3. These “Ice Giants” Have a Hot Secret  Astrobites
  4. Gas from Uranus reveals it has an icy centre  New Scientist
  5. Ice Giants Revisited: Uranus And Neptune As Magma Ocean Worlds  astrobiology.com

item.title: The strawberry moon will soon rise. When to look up. - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. The strawberry moon will soon rise. When to look up.  The Washington Post

  5. A strawberry moon will soon rise. Here's when to look up at June's full moon  10TV

item.title: NASA Rover Finds “Complex Organic Matter” on Mars - Yahoo
item.description:
1. NASA Rover Finds “Complex Organic Matter” on Mars  Yahoo
  2. Nasa rover detects potential signatures of ancient microbial life on Mars  The Guardian
  3. In one drilled Martian rock, Curiosity found 21 organic molecules — seven never before detected on Mars — including a nitrogen-bearing ring structure that belongs to the same chemical family as precursors to RNA and DNA.  Space Daily
  4. NASA Mars rover finds record-breaking trove of organic molecules buried in Bright Angel rock formation  Live Science
  5. Perseverance rover finds even more signs of extinct life on Mars  The Register

item.title: Scientists Tickled Gorillas And Found A 15-Million-Year-Old Clue To How Humans Learned Speech - Forbes
item.description:
1. Scientists Tickled Gorillas And Found A 15-Million-Year-Old Clue To How Humans Learned Speech  Forbes
  2. Rhythm and timing in laughter reveal that human vocal plasticity falls on a hominid continuum | Communications Biology  Nature
  3. To Reveal the Rhythmic Roots of Laughter, Just Tickle an Ape  The New York Times
  4. Get a load of this: Humans and great apes share similar giggles  AP News
  5. What an ape’s laugh can teach us about human language  National Geographic

item.description:
1. How to See the Giant Asteroid That Will Pass by Earth This Weekend  WIRED
  2. A large, harmless asteroid will zip past Earth this weekend  AP News
  3. Watch a 'Potentially hazardous asteroid' the size of a skyscraper close in on Earth live online tonight  Space
  4. Asteroid zooming past Earth on Saturday visible to stargazers  Phys.org

item.title: A 'direct wave' from colliding black holes reveals signature of a whirlpool in spacetime - Phys.org
item.description:
1. A 'direct wave' from colliding black holes reveals signature of a whirlpool in spacetime  Phys.org

  4. Scientists May Have Detected The First Signature of a Black Hole's Event Horizon  ScienceAlert

item.title: Every Homo naledi we know of is female, and the implications are fascinating - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Every Homo naledi we know of is female, and the implications are fascinating  Ars Technica
  2. Fossils of archaic human Homo naledi found in a cave are the same sex. Scientists want to learn why  CNN

  4. Archaic Hominin Species Buried Only Their Women  Nautilus | Science
  5. News - Genetic Material Recovered from 300,000-Year-Old Homo naledi Teeth  Archaeology Magazine

item.title: The Roman Space Telescope has the same size mirror as Hubble, but it will survey the sky up to 1,000 times faster — not because it sees deeper, but because it sees wider: each Roman image captures a patch of sky at least 100 times larger, making Hubble’s - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Roman Space Telescope has the same size mirror as Hubble, but it will survey the sky up to 1,000 times faster — not because it sees deeper, but because it sees wider: each Roman image captures a patch of sky at least 100 times larger, making Hubble’s  Space Daily
  2. Experience the Launch of NASA’s Roman Space Telescope  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida  Spaceflight Now
  4. NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer  Space
  5. Image: Roman Telescope arrives at Kennedy Space Center  Phys.org

item.title: SpaceX won a NASA contract worth up to $843 million to build a single machine whose only job is to drag the space station out of orbit and into the Pacific Ocean - Space Daily
item.description:
1. SpaceX won a NASA contract worth up to $843 million to build a single machine whose only job is to drag the space station out of orbit and into the Pacific Ocean  Space Daily
  2. NASA wants to dump the ISS in the sea. Experts say the plan 'raises serious concerns for ocean health'  Space
  3. The Next Outpost: NASA’s Plans for Replacing the Aging International Space Station  U.S. Government Accountability Office (.gov)
  4. Why does NASA want to crash International Space Station into ocean? | The ISS will be guided to 'Point Nemo' | Inshorts  Inshorts
  5. NASA's Race to Replace ISS Before 2030s Shutdown  Legis1

item.title: In July 2025, a planetary-defence telescope in Chile built to watch for dangerous asteroids accidentally became the instrument that discovered only the third interstellar object ever seen — a comet now thought to be older than the solar system, sweeping throu - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In July 2025, a planetary-defence telescope in Chile built to watch for dangerous asteroids accidentally became the instrument that discovered only the third interstellar object ever seen — a comet now thought to be older than the solar system, sweeping throu  Space Daily

  4. Organisms on 3I/ATLAS? Telltale methane signatures detected: Avi Loeb  NewsNation
  5. Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS is almost as old as the universe itself  Scientific American

item.title: We tend to imagine the Moon as a barren, resourceless rock, but the permanently shadowed craters near its south pole hold something future astronauts may prize more than gold: water ice, confirmed by NASA missions, that could one day be split into oxygen - Space Daily
item.description: We tend to imagine the Moon as a barren, resourceless rock, but the permanently shadowed craters near its south pole hold something future astronauts may prize more than gold: water ice, confirmed by NASA missions, that could one day be split into oxygen  Space Daily

item.title: For nearly forty years, scientists aimed lasers at the Moon and got nothing back from the Soviet rover Lunokhod 1 — then in 2010, a pulse bounced straight back from a reflector that had been sitting in the lunar dust the whole time - Space Daily

item.title: A Russian module called Zvezda has been venting the space station's air through cracks since 2019 — losing nearly 2 kilograms a day before NASA auditors flagged it the highest safety risk aboard - Space Daily
item.description:

  2. Cracks in the International Space Station are causing air leaks – how much longer can it remain habitable?  The Conversation
  3. What Happens If Russia Shuts The Door On Their Leaky ISS Module?  Hackaday
  4. Tension Flared on Space Station as Russia Threatened to Drill and Saw Into Wall, Prompting NASA Astronauts to Take Shelter  Yahoo

item.title: A moon only 10 kilometres wide was hiding around Uranus for decades. Voyager 2 missed it. Hubble missed it. But in 2025, Webb finally caught the faint speck circling near the planet’s inner rings, raising Uranus’s known moon count to 29. - Space Daily
item.description: A moon only 10 kilometres wide was hiding around Uranus for decades. Voyager 2 missed it. Hubble missed it. But in 2025, Webb finally caught the faint speck circling near the planet’s inner rings, raising Uranus’s known moon count to 29.  Space Daily

item.title: Astronauts on the Moon, with no atmosphere above them, looked up and saw a white sun — the yellow colour we see from Earth is not the sun's real colour but an artefact our own atmosphere paints onto it by scattering blue wavelengths sideways into the sky - Space Daily
item.description: Astronauts on the Moon, with no atmosphere above them, looked up and saw a white sun — the yellow colour we see from Earth is not the sun's real colour but an artefact our own atmosphere paints onto it by scattering blue wavelengths sideways into the sky  Space Daily
